title: CI: windows installer flake
body:
https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/actions/runs/26903908539/job/79363394813?pr=28848

There is a problem with the github download, we already added support for using a GITHUB_TOKEN in https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/commit/7c7d56a0fbee78a6a67e0f3b89542969d83ae443

And we pass the token in https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/blob/2d369caca7ca1bc3963cef416e2ab5dc464db701/.github/workflows/ci.yml#L265-L269

Yet it sill fails? Do we loose the env somewhere or maybe the token has the wrong permissions?

cc @l0rd

comments.body: I made a [test](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/28851) to verify what's going on: if the token is invalid, the [function fails](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/actions/runs/26938085841/job/79472564532?pr=28851), so we can assume that the token isn't lost.

Thanks so the env seems to be passed along right, I think we need to give the job explicit contents: read permissions on the token then

https://docs.github.com/en/rest/releases/releases?apiVersion=2026-03-10#get-a-release

> The fine-grained token must have the following permission set:
>
>    "Contents" repository permissions (read)
>
> This endpoint can be used without authentication or the aforementioned permissions if only public resources are requested.
Yes, that's probably it. ~I have also spotted a couple of places where we call the API without auth https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/28852~.
I have updated #28852: I have set the right permissions for the Windows installer job and rolled back the changes to the WebRequest header (those requests weren't using the GH API).

I am afraid we are not done here :(

I saw this just failed on main which should use the latest code https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/actions/runs/27025342094/job/79763827738

the error again claims we are not authenticated, what kind of permissions can this possibly need. Your invalid token test clearly shows this is getting passed to the server.

> I am afraid we are not done here :(
> 
> I saw this just failed on main which should use the latest code https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/actions/runs/27025342094/job/79763827738
> 
> the error again claims we are not authenticated, what kind of permissions can this possibly need. Your invalid token test clearly shows this is getting passed to the server.

The only thing that comes to mind https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/28872 🤷‍♂️
